name: Florida Pharmacist License
description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Florida - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Florida Board of Pharmacy
provider.parentOrganization: Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine

address.description:
Department of Health
P.O. Box 6330
Tallahassee, FL 32314-6330

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Reports
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Florida pharmacist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

mainEntity.name: Is the MPJE required in all states?

acceptedAnswer.text: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

mainEntity.name: Does Florida have reciprocity for pharmacists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Florida?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
